<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants to embed their X (Twitter) feed on their website. There are a few ways to do this:

1. **X/Twitter Embed Timeline Widget** - The official way. You use an `<a>` tag with `class="twitter-timeline"` and include the Twitter widgets.js script. This embeds a scrollable timeline of your tweets.

2. **Individual tweet embeds** - For specific tweets.

The official approach is the simplest and most reliable. Here's how it works:

```html
<a class="twitter-timeline" href="https://twitter.com/HarveenChadha">Tweets by HarveenChadha</a>


```

I'm loading the widgets script to activate the embed...

The standard embed still works with both twitter.com and x.com URLs, so either will function properly.

Now I'm deciding where to place the X feed section—it makes sense to add it as its own section between Teaching & Mentorship and Contact, and I'll need to add a corresponding nav link for it. I'll keep the implementation straightforward by using the Twitter timeline embed widget, which should integrate cleanly with the existing design.</details></think>

---

Yes! The easiest way is to use X's official **embedded timeline widget**. It renders a scrollable feed of your latest posts directly on the page. I see your handle is `@HarveenChadha` from the existing links.

I'll add a new "Latest from X" section right before the Contact section,
